description: B4-tokens benchmark task corpus spec — task selection rationale, per-task hook engagement annotations, and non-engagement records. Updated by WI-609 Slice 1 to add 3 new tasks targeting WI-510-shaved packages (uuid, jsonwebtoken, lodash).

triage_summary.kill_verdict: withdrawn — reclassified as first data point on B4 sweep (see issue #442 comment, 2026-05-13)
triage_summary.csv_parser_correctness: resolved — hook-substitution correctness regression fixed in #450 (closed 2026-05-13); arm-A oracle now matches arm-B baseline
triage_summary.debounce_non_engagement: root-caused in #451, remediated in #454 (both closed 2026-05-13); timer-handle atom seeded; registry rebuild surfaces it as PARTIAL in REGISTRY_COVERAGE.md
triage_summary.open_items: none — all Slice 1 triage actions landed; Slice 2 sweep continues under #167 characterisation initiative

tasks.engagement_notes: High atom density: doubly-linked-list node management, Map operations, and TTL timestamp arithmetic decompose into distinct reusable atoms. The hook has substitution targets across all three sub-patterns. Slice 1 real run showed 22.1% output-token reduction (A=1171, B=1503).

Medium atom density: the CSV state machine is task-specific but delimiter/quoting primitives are generic. Slice 1 real run showed 59.7% output-token reduction (A=391, B=969), though semantic_eq was 0% on Arm A indicating the hook-assisted implementation had oracle failures on edge cases. Reduction measurement is valid but correctness gap exists.

tasks.non_engagement_reason: Debounce is a genuinely novel stateful higher-order function. The Slice 1 bootstrap corpus contains no timer-management atom that the yakccResolve tool could substitute. The model correctly ignored the tool and generated the implementation directly. Both arms produced approximately equal output (~427 vs ~433 tokens, 1.4% noise). Root cause analysis (WI-B4-DEBOUNCE-HOOK-ENGAGEMENT #451): Candidate 1 (registry not seeded for debounce-shaped contracts) confirmed — the Slice 1 registry does not contain a debounce atom. Candidate 2 (embedding match threshold) and Candidate 3 (substitution flow logic bug) are ruled out: the model DID see the yakccResolve tool (Arm A input_tokens=1226 vs Arm B input_tokens=567, delta=659 tokens = system prompt suffix + tool schema) but chose not to invoke it because no matching atom exists. This is correct behavior: the hook should not hallucinate atom names. Resolution: document non-engagement here. Path to engagement in Slice 2: seed a timer-management atom (setTimeout/clearTimeout closure pattern) into the registry. The debounce prompt would then have a candidate for at least the timer-state primitive, reducing the code the model must regenerate.

tasks.engagement_notes: High atom density: uuid Slice 4 (WI-510 S4) shipped two distinct atoms — v4.js (generation, 35 LoC of byte-manipulation with crypto RNG + version/variant bit-setting) and validate.js (~15 LoC regex-based canonical form check). The hook has direct substitution targets for both generateV4 and validateV4. A hook-equipped LLM should be able to substitute both atoms and avoid regenerating ~80-100 LoC of byte-fiddling. Engagement prediction: high. Measured engagement: TBD (post-rerun, operator-appended). WI-510 S4 atom paths: packages/seeds/uuid/v4.js, packages/seeds/uuid/validate.js.

High atom density: jsonwebtoken Slice 6 (WI-510 S6) shipped two atoms — verify.js (HMAC-SHA256 verify logic with algorithm check + timingSafeEqual + exp validation) and decode.js (base64url decode + header/payload parse). The hook should substitute both atoms: the verify path covers the full verification sequence, decode.js covers base64url + JSON parsing. A hook-naive LLM regenerates ~150-200 LoC of HMAC + base64url plumbing including the algorithm-confusion vulnerability. Engagement prediction: high. Measured engagement: TBD (post-rerun, operator-appended). WI-510 S6 atom paths: packages/seeds/jsonwebtoken/verify.js, packages/seeds/jsonwebtoken/decode.js.

High atom density: lodash Slice 7 (WI-510 S7) shipped two atoms — cloneDeep.js (top-level clone entry, 30 LoC wrapping _baseClone) and _baseClone.js (~150 LoC recursive type-dispatch with WeakMap cycle detection, Date/RegExp/Map/Set/typed-array handlers, prototype chain preservation, symbol-key enumeration). The hook should substitute both: the outer cloneDeep atom wraps the inner _baseClone atom. A hook-naive LLM regenerates 100-200 LoC of recursive type-dispatch, often omitting cycles, symbol keys, or prototype chain. Note: debounce-with-cancel also uses lodash shave but for a different atom (timer closure pattern); this task targets the cloneDeep/baseClone atoms specifically. Engagement prediction: high. Measured engagement: TBD (post-rerun, operator-appended). WI-510 S7 atom paths: packages/seeds/lodash/cloneDeep.js, packages/seeds/lodash/_baseClone.js.

notes: hook_engagement values: 'none' = model did not use yakccResolve tool; 'low'/'medium'/'high' = tool used, engagement quality varies
non_engagement_reason is required when hook_engagement is 'none'
follow_up_issue references GitHub issue number for tracking; null if no follow-up needed

WI-609 Slice 1 (2026-05-16): +3 tasks added targeting WI-510 S4/S6/S7 shaved atoms. corpus-spec.json n_tasks updated from 3 to 11 (all tasks in tasks.json are now annotated here). engagement_notes for new tasks are predictions; measured values appended post-rerun.
